project.title: Co-IP-MS identify candidate host factors that interact with CHIKV nsP4
project.projectDescription: The Chikungunya virus (CHIKV) is a single positive-stranded RNA virus transmitted by mosquitoes. More than 40 countries have reported cases of CHIKV infection to varying degrees. Currently, there is no licensed specific antiviral drug or vaccine for CHIKV in the world. In this study, we have identified host protein interactors of CHIKV nsP4 proteins in 293T cells and the major cellular pathways involved in the interaction between host factors and CHIKV nsp4 using Co-Immunoprecipitation and GST Pull-down followed by high-throughput quantitative mass spectrometry.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Co-IP was performed via Flag-tag Protein IP Assay Kit with Magnetic Beads (Beyotime, China) according to the recommended manufacture’s protocol. Briefly, IP lysis solution was prepared as a mixture of Lysis Buffer and Protease Inhibitor Cocktail (100X) at the ratio of 100:1. TBS (10X) is diluted with ultra-pure water to obtain 1X TBS. According to the ratio of 20μl magnetic beads suspension per 500μl protein lysate, magnetic beads were prepared and washed twice on magnetic rack with TBS. The cell lysate was prepared by resuspending cell pellet with 150μl IP lysis solution and incubated on ice for 10min, followed by centrifugation at 12000rpm for 10min. The supernatant was collected and incubated with BeyoMagTM Mouse IgG Magnetic Beads in a rotating mixer at 4℃ for 1 hour to get rid of the non-specific proteins. The mixture was separated on magnetic rack and the supernatant was ready for the Co-IP experiment. BeyoMagTM Anti-Flag Magnetic Beads were added to the supernatant in the ratio of 20μl magnetic beads per 500μl lysate, which was incubated in a rotating mixer at 4℃ overnight. After incubation, the mixture was separated on magnetic rack and the supernatant was discarded. Magnetic beads were resuspended with 500μl IP cracking solution to get rid of nonspecifically bound proteins, which was repeated for three times. 100μl Acid Elution Buffer for every 20μl original magnetic beads was added to elute co-immunoprecipitated proteins by incubation at room temperature in a rotating mixer for 5 min. The mixture was separated on a magnetic rack for 10 sec. The supernatant was transferred to the new Ep tube, and 10μl Neutralization Buffer was immediately added to the co-immunoprecipitated proteins that is ready for further analysis.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Mass spectrometry analysis was conducted by the company APTBIO (Shanghai, China). Briefly, samples were firstly treated with reagents for reduction and alkylation, followed by enzymatic hydrolysis at 37℃ for 20 hours. The hydrolyzed samples were freeze-dried after desalting, dissolved in 0.1%FA solution, and stored at -20℃  for use. Samples were loaded to the chromatographic Trap column balanced with 95% liquid A (0.1% formic acid aqueous solution) for mass spectrometry analysis. The raw file of mass spectrometry test (raw file) uses the search engine Proteome Discoverer 2.5 to search the corresponding database, and finally obtains the identified protein results. Search database parameters: enzyme (Trypsin), database (uniprot_Homo_ sapiens_ 203800_ 20220104), fixed modification (Carbamidomethy1 (C)), variable modification (Carbamidomethy1 (M)), the maximum missing site was 2, and the confidence of the screening peptide was high.
